title: elaeocarpus polydactylus
intro: Elaeocarpus polydactylus, also known as the "Tall Panakan", is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in the tropical regions of Malaysia, Thailand, and the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. Elaeocarpus polydactylus is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal infusion or decoction, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
